You can use the click context to detect CLI mode.
The flask command passes a context object to your function.
You can check if the context is a CLI invocation.
This allows you to skip the environment variable entirely.
Your create_app function can detect the invocation type.
You might pass a flag directly to the factory function.
The CLI loader can be configured to set this flag.
This approach avoids the need for manual variable setting.
You can define a custom command that calls your factory.
The standard flask run command will not trigger this.
This separation keeps your server and CLI logic distinct.
You can also use the click context to pass data.
The context object holds information about the command.
You can inspect the command name to decide logic.
This method is cleaner than using environment variables.
It ensures your app knows how it was started.
The factory pattern supports this kind of flexibility well.
You can create a specific config for CLI usage.
This config can be loaded when the CLI is active.
Your routes will remain untouched by this change.
The CLI commands will get the special configuration.
This solution satisfies your requirement for no extra input.
